text: [serious] What happened to Rohan happens to thousands of students every week. [focused] Now you're going to walk through the scams yourself — and learn to spot their tricks in real time.
[tense] 5 messages are waiting for you. Some are fine. Some will try to take everything. [challenging] Can you tell the difference?
[shocked] That's actually him. The voice. The face. Everything.
[skeptical] But... Virat Kohli doing a UPI giveaway on a random link?
[suspicious] And why does the channel only have 3 videos?
[confused] I haven't done anything.
[anxious] But what if people believe it?
[worried] What if the school sees it?
[uneasy] They said don't tell anyone.
[confused] 847291... that code came to MY phone though.
[suspicious] Why would their account send to me?
[hesitant] They seem nice. But.
[realizing] Wait.
[thinking fast] Kabir. Ranked match. He'd be panicking.
[suspicious] But this isn't his number.
[alert] And why would his OTP come to me?
[realizing] Wait — didn't I just see this same setup?
[panicked] Banned. 15 minutes. I've spent two years on this account.
[stressed] I haven't done anything. But what if they made a mistake?
[afraid] What if I don't reply and I lose everything?
[doubtful] Wait. Would real Krafton staff ever...?
[calm] ok. you just walked through 5 scenarios. real ones. messages that get sent to thousands of students every single day.
[reassuring] some of you got all of them. some didn't. both are fine. that's what this is for — so it happens here first.
[clear] three big patterns: deepfakes and AI scams, OTP scams, and gaming and social tricks. same tricks, different apps.
[confident] now let me show you the patterns underneath all of it. once you see them, you'll never unsee them.
[serious] one thing is underneath all of this. every single scenario.
[emphatic] urgency.
[warning] '5 minutes.' '87 spots left.' 'Permanent ban.' 'Quick bro.' '2 hours or we send it.'
[grounded] that feeling of rush — the slight panic in your chest — that is not a reason to act faster. that is the warning. [emphatic] that feeling IS the trick.
[firm] whenever something feels urgent and you didn't start it — stop completely. [reassuring] that pause is your defence.
[resolved] i'm sending these scam cards to Rohan — he needs to see this
[approving] yeah. send him the whole lesson.
[understanding] ok. I get it now. Rohan didn't do anything wrong. [serious] they built the whole thing to catch him.
[affirming] exactly. and now you know the build.
[sheepish] i failed the speed round lol. [admitting] the deepfake one got me
[proud] i found all 4 things in the email 👀
[clear] here. the 5 things you keep. forever.
[confident] Here come the scam cards — tap through each one.
[serious] That video was AI-generated. Virat Kohli never made it.
[warning] Scammers used deepfake technology — AI that can copy anyone's face and voice from existing videos.
[clear] The channel was created 4 days ago. Real celebrities don't do giveaways through random links.
[focused] Your UPI PIN was the only thing they needed.
[serious] Scammers build fake urgency — 'only 87 spots' — specifically so you share it before thinking.
[grounded] This is exactly what always happens.
[warning] Paying tells them it's working. They don't stop — they ask for more.
[focused] The content they threatened you with? Almost certainly AI-generated and fake.
[serious] They send the same message to hundreds of students. Paying never ends it.
[firm] Don't engage. Every reply tells them the account is active and the person is scared.
[serious] They use that to push harder.
[confident] Silence and reporting is stronger than any reply.
[focused] They went to Free Fire's login page and typed YOUR username.
[clear] Free Fire sent a verification code — to YOU, the real owner.
[serious] They pretended it was a mistake.
[warning] That code was the key to YOUR account.
[grounded] There is no 'accidental number'. That story doesn't exist.
[firm] Do not engage. If an OTP came to your phone, it is for your account.
[clear] They can sign up with their own number without your involvement.
[serious] Replying keeps the scam active — there is no accidental number story.
[focused] Someone found out your friend's name and used it.
[grounded] That's all it took.
[serious] They knew that you'd trust Kabir. So they became Kabir.
[warning] The name was real. The number wasn't.
[serious] They'll say 'I told you, it's Kabir, just send it.'
[grounded] Asking for proof in a panic situation rarely works — they'll have an answer.
[confident] The only real verification is calling his actual number.
[clear] There was no investigation. No software detected. No ban.
[serious] The threat was fiction. The urgency was the weapon.
[warning] 'Reply in 15 minutes' was designed specifically to stop you from thinking clearly.
[focused] Krafton has full access to their own servers. They will NEVER need your password.
[firm] No real company ever will.
[warning] They now know your email.
[focused] They'll try it on 10 other sites.
[serious] Partial information is still information.
[grounded] Half a key can still open a lock.
[serious] Virat Kohli's real channel has been active for years and has tens of millions of subscribers.
[clear] This was a deepfake — an AI-generated video designed to look and sound exactly like a real person.
[grounded] One check. Entire scam exposed.
[focused] What to look for: new channel with few videos, UPI/PIN requests via external links, fake urgency, celebrities never do random-link giveaways.
[serious] This type of scam is called sextortion. It targets teenagers specifically.
[clear] The photos or videos they claim to have are almost always AI-generated fakes.
[grounded] They send this same threat to hundreds of people — it's a template.
[warning] Paying never makes it stop. It makes it worse.
[reassuring] Telling a trusted adult is not weakness — it's the exact right move.
[affirming] You have done nothing wrong. The shame belongs entirely to them.
[supportive] You cannot handle this alone — and you shouldn't have to.
[clear] OTPs only go to the account owner's registered number.
[focused] If the code came to you — the account is yours.
[warning] This is the most used trick to steal gaming accounts.
[confident] You just blocked it completely.
[clear] One phone call. That's the entire defence.
[grounded] When a 'friend' contacts you from an unknown number asking for something urgently — call the real number first.
[firm] Always.
[serious] This is the hardest one — because the stakes feel real.
[clear] But here's the truth: Krafton would never ban you through an in-game message and demand your password.
[focused] If you were genuinely flagged, they'd act from their systems — silently.
[firm] The moment someone asks for your password, the answer is always no.
